danger_signals.visual_form: Animated soldier sprites with hostile posture
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat; player troops vulnerable to single-round kills

danger_signals.visual_form: Jeeps, tanks, helicopters rendered from overhead perspective
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level; requires strategic positioning and special weapons

danger_signals.visual_form: Missile-armed turrets and buildings on map
danger_signals.meaning: Stationary but persistent threats; require explosive weapons to destroy

danger_signals.visual_form: Rivers (slow-movement zones), quicksand, mines visualized on ground
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger; reduces soldier effectiveness or causes casualties

danger_signals.visual_form: Limited supply crates scattered across map
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity creates mission failure risk if weapons depleted

danger_signals.visual_form: Small squad sizes (max 6 soldiers) versus numerous enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Outnumbered condition; player is in constant tactical disadvantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Crate objects placed strategically on map
safety_signals.meaning: Resource replenishment possible; restores grenades and rocket ammunition
Safe zones for reorganization and split-squad reunification

safety_signals.visual_form: Jungle, snow, desert environmental features provide visual obstruction
safety_signals.meaning: Natural barriers reduce enemy line-of-sight; temporary respite from fire

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player troops distinguished by controllable unit sprites; appear more organized than enemy forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Squad composition changes per mission; visible as grouped units under player cursor control

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Generic hostile infantry, vehicles, and installations; power scaling increases across 24 missions (72 levels)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy soldiers spawn from buildings; vehicles patrol designated routes; turrets occupy static positions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player troops possess superior firepower relative to enemy infantry
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Soldiers respond quickly to commands; enemy infantry falls easily to machine gun fire

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy infantry becomes visually more threatening; vehicle and installation density increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy units require more rounds to kill; coordinated vehicle/turret attacks become frequent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dense foliage, vegetation rendering; overhead view maintains tactical clarity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Terrain provides natural cover; movement speed unchanged but visual concealment possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: White/arctic color palette; igloos visible on terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Snowmobile vehicles appear; terrain otherwise traversable with standard speed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sandy/tan color palette; arid landscape rendering
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard unit behavior; unique terrain hazards (quicksand) present
Soldiers move slower while crossing rivers; unable to fire during water traversal; vulnerability window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visible depletion of supply crates; fewer explosive weapon pickups available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player forced to use grenades/rockets conservatively or risk mission failure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visible reduction in on-screen soldier count; squad size diminishes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Remaining troops operate with reduced firepower; tactical positioning becomes more critical

environmental_storytelling.example: Rural level design inspired by Emmerdale Farm; each biome signals different mission context and unique hazards
Buildings spawn infantry; turrets occupy high-ground/defensive positions; vehicle patrols suggest organized enemy military structure
Rivers, quicksand, mines embedded in landscape communicate hostile environment beyond enemy forces; world itself is dangerous
Mission-by-mission squad size changes (max 6 soldiers) suggest escalating difficulty and resource constraints of ongoing campaign
Limited ammunition in supply crates implies logistical challenge; player must conserve explosives, creating strategic resource tension

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responds to point-and-click commands; moves to designated location; fires on nearest enemy
Movement speed reduced; firing disabled; vulnerability increased
Can be killed by single round from machine gun; fragile despite superior early-game firepower
Emerges from installations when proximity triggered or building active; infinite spawn capacity until building destroyed
Returns fire; difficulty/damage output scales with mission progression
Follows predetermined route; presents mobile threat; immune to standard machine gun fire in some variants
Stationary; missile-armed; fires at player units within range; invulnerable to machine gun fire
Can be divided into smaller groups for flanking, resource conservation, or risk mitigation

notes: Cannon Fodder communicates world-state primarily through: (1) enemy composition scaling—early missions show outnumbered but superior player force, late missions invert this through vehicle/installation density; (2) environmental hazards embedded in terrain (rivers, quicksand, mines) that function as secondary threat layer independent of enemy AI; (3) mission setting variety (jungle/snow/desert/rural) that signals campaign progression through visual biome shifts; (4) overhead perspective consistently reveals threat topology, maintaining player tactical awareness; (5) squad fragility (single-round kills, small squad sizes) visually communicates anti-war tone through gameplay mechanic—soldiers are expendable cannon fodder. The game lacks faction sigils or color-coded allegiances; instead, threat is communicated through sprite behavior, weapon loadout visibility, and positioning. Resource scarcity (ammunition limitations) is the primary political/strategic signal—survival depends on conservation, not overwhelming force.
